description: Machine-readable catalog of AINL adapters for OpenClaw integration

core.description: Built-in arithmetic, string, date/time, and control utilities

id.notes: Identity / object literal materialization (same as echo for values).

http.description: HTTP client with host allowlist and response size limits
notes: Performs up to 3 attempts with small exponential backoff on transport errors (URLError/timeouts) and 5xx HTTP status codes.
Does not retry 4xx client errors; those fail immediately with a status error.

a2a.description: Agent-to-Agent (A2A) client — Agent Card discovery and JSON-RPC tasks/send, tasks/get (ArmaraOS-compatible wire)

send.notes: JSON-RPC tasks/send; session_id optional; timeout_s overrides default

discover_hermes.notes: Reads HERMES_HOME or ~/.hermes/a2a.json for base_url, then GET {base}/.well-known/agent.json. Uses relaxed local URL checks for the operator-controlled file.

send_hermes.notes: Loads Hermes a2a.json (base_url + optional send_binding: auto | armaraos_jsonrpc | a2a_http). Tries ArmaraOS JSON-RPC tasks/send when card.url matches base_url origin, then Linux Foundation HTTP POST …/message:send (and supportedInterfaces[].url/message:send).

notes: Block loopback and private host literals unless allow_insecure_local is set.
When allow_hosts is non-empty, hostname must be listed before outbound calls.
strict_ssrf: resolve DNS; reject if any address is private/loopback unless allow_insecure_local (see docs/integrations/A2A_ADAPTER.md).
follow_redirects false by default; when true each redirect is re-validated.

sqlite.description: SQLite database access with optional table allowlist
rules: query target requires SELECT statements
execute target requires DML/DDL
table allowlist enforced by regex

postgres.description: PostgreSQL database access with allowlist and transaction support
rules: query target requires SELECT/CTE statements
execute target requires DML/DDL and allow_write=true
transaction executes ordered query/execute operations atomically
parameterized queries are required for untrusted inputs

mysql.description: MySQL database access with allowlist and transaction support
rules: query target requires SELECT/CTE statements
execute target requires DML/DDL and allow_write=true
transaction executes ordered query/execute operations atomically
parameterized queries are required for untrusted inputs

redis.description: Redis cache/session/queue/pubsub access with optional key-prefix policy
rules: write verbs require allow_write=true
optional allow_prefixes policy scopes keys/channels
transaction executes ordered redis operations atomically
native async mode uses redis.asyncio for full verb parity with bounded subscribe polling

dynamodb.description: AWS DynamoDB access for item, query/scan, batch, and transactional operations
rules: mutating verbs require allow_write=true
table allowlist enforced across all table-scoped operations
transact_write and batch_write respect the same write gate
streams.subscribe uses bounded polling with timeout/max_events and follows table allowlist
native async mode supports non-blocking stream batching via background listener tasks

airtable.description: Airtable REST adapter for no-code style table/record workflows
rules: mutating verbs require allow_write=true
table allowlist enforced when configured
attachment download/upload-url can be constrained by allow_attachment_hosts
adapter applies bounded retries for rate-limit/transient http failures
webhook registration returns metadata only; webhook payload processing is external to this adapter

supabase.description: Supabase convenience wrapper with Postgres delegation and auth/storage/realtime helpers
rules: db/table operations delegate to postgres adapter semantics
mutating operations require allow_write=true
table, bucket, and channel allowlists are enforced when configured
realtime_broadcast and realtime_ack require allow_write=true
realtime events normalize to {event,schema,table,record,old_record?,sequence?,timestamp?}
realtime replay/fanout/cursor helpers are lightweight in-process primitives
All paths must stay within sandbox_root
Extension allowlist enforced when set
Size limits enforced for read/write

email.description: Email integration supporting IMAP/SMTP and MCP providers (Gmail, Outlook, etc.)

send.side_effect: sends email via SMTP or MCP with optional attachments
notes: body can be string (plain text) or dict {html, text} for HTML email
attachments can be list of file paths or list of {path, filename, content, content_type} dicts
HTML emails use multipart/alternative with plain text fallback

read.returns: [{id, from, to, subject, body, body_html?, timestamp, unread, message_id, in_reply_to?, references?, attachments?}]
read.side_effect: reads emails via IMAP or MCP
notes: Returns message_id, in_reply_to, references for threading
When include_attachments=true, attachments array contains {filename, content_type, size, content} (base64)
body_html included when email has HTML version

search.returns: [{id, from, to, subject, snippet, timestamp, message_id}]
search.side_effect: searches emails via provider-specific query syntax

reply.side_effect: replies to email with proper threading headers (In-Reply-To, References)
notes: Fetches original message to extract headers and recipients
Sets In-Reply-To and References for proper email threading
reply_all includes all original recipients except sender
body can be string or {html, text} dict
✅ FULLY IMPLEMENTED: Send, read, search, reply (with threading), and draft management
✅ HTML email support: Use dict {html, text} as body for multipart/alternative format
✅ Attachments: Send/receive any file type with automatic MIME type detection
✅ Email threading: Reply preserves In-Reply-To and References headers for conversation continuity
✅ Draft management: Save drafts to IMAP Drafts folder, update by draft_id
Supports IMAP/SMTP (any provider) and MCP integrations (Gmail, Outlook)
Provider parameter auto-detects best available backend
Search query syntax varies by provider (Gmail: 'from:user@example.com', IMAP: 'SUBJECT "message"')
Authentication: Use app-specific passwords for Gmail (not main account password)
Security: All connections use TLS/SSL encryption (STARTTLS for SMTP, SSL for IMAP)
Legacy G target maintained for OpenClaw backward compatibility

calendar.returns: List of upcoming calendar events with fields: id, title, start, end, location

social.description: OpenClaw web search for mentions (only G target)
social.returns: List of mention objects with fields: id, text, ts

db.returns: List of lead objects with enriched fields (name, city, phone, website, etc.)

fanout.description: Parallel execution of multiple adapter calls via the runtime registry

ALL.notes: Each plan is [adapter, target, ...args]; requires _adapter_registry in call context.

queue.description: Notification queue (sends to self via OpenClaw)
rules: Only queue 'notify' is implemented for the current monitors.

Payload is formatter-driven and tolerant; examples include fields like email_count, cal_count, social_count, leads_count, health_score, failed_services, ts, but this shape is advisory rather than a strict schema.

extras.description: Utility health checks: file + Docker + HTTP + backup mtime + metrics summaries

metrics.returns: {run_count, ok_count, error_count, ok_ratio, runtime_versions, result_kinds, trace_op_counts, label_counts, timestamps_present}

agent.description: Local mailbox coordination for agent-to-agent tasks. Minimal surface: send_task and read_result only; no polling, discovery, or list operations.
notes: No read_task or list_agents verbs.
Task consumption and result creation are external/orchestrator-driven.

tiktok.returns: F returns report objects; videos returns video objects with fields: id, tiktokId, title, description, processedAt, createdAt

memory.description: Extension-level memory adapter for explicit execution-facing records keyed by (namespace, record_kind, record_id).
notes: Extension/OpenClaw-only memory adapter; not part of the canonical AINL core.
Records are typed by namespace and record_kind; record_id selects instances.
payload must be a JSON object; ttl_seconds is an advisory hint only.
append is intended for log-like records (e.g. daily_log.note) and will fail on non-log payloads.

vector_memory.description: Local JSON chunk store with keyword overlap scoring (stub semantic search; embeddings can replace scoring later).
notes: Canonical dotted: R vector_memory.SEARCH "q" [limit] ->out; upsert uses split form R vector_memory upsert ... ->out.
No network; no extra dependencies.

ainl_graph_memory.description: JSON-backed graph memory for bridge workflows; runtime dispatches IR MemoryRecall / MemorySearch and GraphPatch (memory.patch) to this adapter namespace.
notes: Strict dotted verbs: MEMORY_RECALL, MEMORY_SEARCH, MEMORY_PATCH; runtime/engine.py GraphPatch uses adapters.call target graph_patch (bridge also accepts memory_patch as an alias).
Operator contract: docs/adapters/AINL_GRAPH_MEMORY.md.

tool_registry.description: Local JSON registry for named tools (metadata + optional JSON schema).
notes: DISCOVER returns a TOOL.discover-style envelope.
Use split form: R tool_registry list / discover / get name / register ... ->out.

audit_trail.description: Append-only JSONL audit trail adapter for regulated teams (compliance logging; no network).
notes: This adapter only writes when your graph invokes it (e.g. R audit_trail.record {...}). Enabling the adapter alone is not enough.
Supported sinks: file://, syslog://, stderr://, stdout:// (see adapters/audit_trail.py).
Best-effort redaction: sensitive keys are replaced with [REDACTED].

ptc_runner.description: Optional external PTC Runner bridge for executing PTC-Lisp snippets from AINL graphs.
notes: Lowercase split form and dotted uppercase form are both supported: R ptc_runner run ... and R ptc_runner.RUN ...
Health aliases are supported: R ptc_runner health and R ptc_runner status.
For LangGraph hybrids, use intelligence/ptc_to_langgraph_bridge.py to generate thin bridge nodes without modifying the core emitter.
Adapter remains opt-in and is not active unless explicitly enabled.

llm_query.description: Optional ad-hoc LLM query adapter for prompt execution from AINL graph steps.
notes: Supports split and dotted forms: R llm_query query ... and R llm_query.QUERY ...
Underscore-prefixed context keys are filtered before request serialization.

solana.description: Solana JSON-RPC adapter: native/SPL transfers, raw program invoke, account reads, event listen simulation

get_pyth_price.returns: {ok, parser: legacy|v2, price, conf, confidence, timestamp?, ...}

hermes_fallback.returns: {ok, parser: hermes, price, conf, timestamp, exponent, ...}

notes: Requires optional Python packages solana and solders; SPL transfer may require spl.token bindings.
Use strict dotted verbs: R solana.GET_ACCOUNT ..., R solana.GET_BALANCE ..., R solana.TRANSFER ...
First blockchain.solana family member; registry id is solana for strict compatibility.
GET_PYTH_PRICE optional second arg expect_update_account (bool): tries PriceUpdateV2 then legacy PriceAccount; unified parser field; Hermes for off-chain only via HERMES_FALLBACK.
HERMES_FALLBACK feed_id_hex (64 hex chars): Hermes /v2/updates/price/latest; optional AINL_PYTH_HERMES_URL base override.
DERIVE_PDA seeds_json (JSON array of UTF-8 strings) + program_id: Pubkey.find_program_address; dry-run returns mock address.
INVOKE optional trailing priority_fee_microlamports: micro-lamports per compute unit (SetComputeUnitPrice), not whole-lamport tips.

embedding_memory.description: Semantic search with vector embeddings - sidecar index linking to memory adapter records
notes: Sidecar semantic index: embeddings link to memory adapter (namespace, kind, id) refs
Memory adapter remains source of truth for actual data
Modes: stub=hash vectors (no network), local=hash-based (better than stub), openai=real embeddings
Returns top-k results by cosine similarity score
Use with memory adapter for RAG: index with UPSERT_REF, search with SEARCH/QUERY

code_context.description: Code indexing with tiered chunking for LLM context (inspired by ctxzip)

COMPRESS_CONTEXT.side_effect: ranked compression with TF-IDF or embeddings

notes: Tiered chunking: Tier 0 (signatures), Tier 1 (signatures+summaries), Tier 2 (full source)
PageRank-style importance scoring for chunks
Token-budget packing with greedy selection
COMPRESS_CONTEXT uses embedding_memory adapter for semantic ranking if available, else TF-IDF
Import graph dependencies and reverse impact analysis
Inspired by BradyD2003/ctxzip and chrismicah/forgeindex

langchain_tool.description: Invoke LangChain-compatible tools from AINL programs
notes: Dynamic tool registration via Python: register_langchain_tool(name, tool)
Tool name can also be used as verb: R langchain_tool.my_tool "arg" ->result
Requires langchain-core package (optional dependency)
Built-in demo stub: my_search_tool
Enable with --enable-adapter langchain_tool

llm.description: LLM provider bridge - unified interface to OpenRouter, Anthropic, OpenAI, Ollama, Cohere
notes: Unified LLM interface with fallback chains
Cost tracking via intelligence.monitor.cost_tracker
Providers: OpenRouter, Anthropic, OpenAI, Ollama (local), Cohere
Circuit breaker for failed providers
Configure via llm.fallback_chain in config

ext.description: Extensions adapter - echo passthrough and optional subprocess execution
notes: Echo returns args[0] if present, else target (legacy test compatibility)
EXEC/RUN disabled by default for security - requires explicit opt-in
Subprocess timeout configurable via environment

web.description: Web scraping and content fetching (OpenClaw integration)

G.side_effect: fetches web content from OpenClaw service

notes: OpenClaw-specific web search integration
Returns web content for AI processing
Requires OpenClaw backend service

tools.description: Tool invocation bridge for external tool systems
notes: Bridge adapter for tool execution systems
Integrates with runtime tool registry
Used by executor bridge for tool calls
Low-level bridge for runtime interop
Used internally by executor systems
Not typically called directly from AINL programs

api.description: Generic API adapter for external service calls
notes: Generic HTTP API wrapper
Use http adapter for simple requests
api adapter for structured API calls with authentication
Generic authentication adapter
Provider-specific implementations available
Session token management
Use for multi-step authenticated workflows

txn.description: Transaction management adapter for multi-step operations
notes: Transaction coordination for multi-step operations
Use with database adapters for ACID guarantees
Tracks transaction state across adapter calls

github.description: GitHub API integration for repository operations
notes: GitHub REST API v3 integration
Requires GITHUB_TOKEN for authenticated requests
Rate limiting applies (5000 requests/hour authenticated)

crm.description: CRM operations for customer relationship management
notes: Generic CRM adapter
Stores data in local database (crm_db.py)
Extensible for external CRM integrations (Salesforce, HubSpot, etc.)
All adapters are optional except 'core'. Enable only what's needed.
Side effects are explicit: cache writes, network calls, and OpenClaw integrations are marked.
For small offline models: focus on core, http, sqlite, fs, and cache. The OpenClaw-specific adapters (email, calendar, social, db, queue, svc) are specialized.
Error handling: adapter errors throw; catch with If/J blocks or use safe wrappers.

agent.description: Local mailbox coordination protocol for agent-to-agent tasks. Minimal surface: send_task and read_result only.
